---
title: "Hermosillo 2010 Case Study"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Hermosillo 2010 Case Study}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

This vignette reproduces the Hermosillo 2010 calibration study using the
package datasets and the script under `inst/scripts/`.

```{r}
library(twostraindengue)

config <- load_hermosillo_2010_analysis_config()
config$observed_rows <- 1:3
config$grids$time$full <- seq(0, 5, by = 0.5)
config$grids$time$calibration <- seq(0, 2, by = 0.5)
config$grids$time$fit <- seq(0, 5, by = 0.5)
config$grids$parameters$beta_h <- seq(1, 1.4, length.out = 3)
config$grids$parameters$beta_m <- seq(0.001, 0.003, length.out = 3)
config$grids$parameters$r01 <- seq(1.7, 1.9, length.out = 3)
config$grids$parameters$r02 <- seq(0.003, 0.0034, length.out = 3)
config$grids$parameters$r0 <- seq(1.3, 1.4, length.out = 3)
config$outputs$write_outputs <- FALSE
config$outputs$show_progress <- FALSE

analysis <- run_hermosillo_2010_analysis(config = config)

analysis$reproduction_numbers
```

For the full-resolution study outputs, use the packaged script
`inst/scripts/run_hermosillo_2010_analysis.R`, which calls the same exported
workflow with production-sized grids and file output enabled.
